explicit_text: I was born and raised in Bhutan, spending the first 13 to 14 years of my life there. I grew up in a very rural countryside where my parents were farmers, and I walked two hours to school every day. During my childhood, Bhutan was undergoing political turmoil, especially with the government's ethnic cleansing policies against Nepali-speaking people. Despite the challenges, my early years in Bhutan were filled with family support and a close-knit community.
implicit_text: I was born and raised in a small Himalayan kingdom, spending the first 13 to 14 years of my life there. I grew up in a very rural countryside where my parents were farmers, and I walked two hours to school every day. During my childhood, this kingdom was undergoing political turmoil, especially with the government's ethnic cleansing policies against Nepali-speaking people. Despite the challenges, my early years in that land were filled with family support and a close-knit community.

explicit_text: Though I was originally from Bhutan, I now live in Columbus, Ohio. This city represents a new chapter in my life after fleeing Bhutan due to political persecution. Columbus has been my home where I have settled after the difficult journey and displacement from my birthplace.
implicit_text: Though I was originally from a Himalayan kingdom, I now live in the capital city of Ohio. This city represents a new chapter in my life after fleeing my homeland due to political persecution. It has been my home where I have settled after the difficult journey and displacement from my birthplace.

explicit_text: After being forced to leave Bhutan in 1992 due to ethnic cleansing, my family and I fled to Nepal seeking refuge. We traveled through India and finally reached Nepal, where there were already refugee camps sponsored by UNHCR. Although we spoke the same language, life in Nepal was uncertain and challenging as we had lost everything and had to start over as refugees.
implicit_text: After being forced to leave my Himalayan homeland in 1992 due to ethnic cleansing, my family and I fled to a neighboring South Asian country seeking refuge. We traveled through India and finally reached this country, where there were already refugee camps sponsored by an international agency. Although we spoke the same language, life there was uncertain and challenging as we had lost everything and had to start over as refugees.

explicit_text: During my childhood in Bhutan, the King of Bhutan implemented policies aimed at ethnic cleansing of Nepali-speaking people. The King wanted these people to either leave the country or fully assimilate by adopting Bhutanese dress, culture, and Buddhism. This royal policy deeply affected my family and community, leading to our eventual forced displacement.
implicit_text: During my childhood in the Himalayan kingdom, the monarch implemented policies aimed at ethnic cleansing of Nepali-speaking people. The ruler wanted these people to either leave the country or fully assimilate by adopting the local dress, culture, and dominant religion. This royal policy deeply affected my family and community, leading to our eventual forced displacement.

explicit_text: I come from a Brahmin family in Bhutan, which shaped much of my religious upbringing. My parents taught us religious rituals and we recited the Gita together every morning. Being Brahmin meant that religion was an essential part of my daily life and identity, even though I sometimes felt conflicted about the social stratification it represented.
implicit_text: I come from a high-caste priestly family in the Himalayan kingdom, which shaped much of my religious upbringing. My parents taught us religious rituals and we recited a sacred Hindu scripture together every morning. Being from this caste meant that religion was an essential part of my daily life and identity, even though I sometimes felt conflicted about the social stratification it represented.

explicit_text: The Gita was a central part of my religious education growing up in Bhutan. Every morning, my siblings, cousins, and I would sit with my father to recite the Gita as part of our daily rituals. This practice helped instill values about life, humanity, and service that have stayed with me throughout my life.
implicit_text: A sacred Hindu scripture was a central part of my religious education growing up in the Himalayan kingdom. Every morning, my siblings, cousins, and I would sit with my father to recite it as part of our daily rituals. This practice helped instill values about life, humanity, and service that have stayed with me throughout my life.

explicit_text: The ethnic cleansing in Bhutan during the early 1990s was a traumatic event that forced my family and many others to flee. The government targeted Nepali-speaking people, accusing them of being foreigners and forcing them to abandon their culture or leave. I witnessed violence, intimidation, and destruction in my village, which ultimately led to our escape to Nepal in 1992.
implicit_text: The ethnic cleansing in the Himalayan kingdom during the early 1990s was a traumatic event that forced my family and many others to flee. The government targeted Nepali-speaking people, accusing them of being foreigners and forcing them to abandon their culture or leave. I witnessed violence, intimidation, and destruction in my village, which ultimately led to our escape to a neighboring country in 1992.

explicit_text: Nepali was the language spoken by my family and community in Bhutan, but during the ethnic cleansing, speaking Nepali became a reason for discrimination. We were told to abandon our language and culture to be accepted as Bhutanese. Despite this pressure, Nepali remained a vital part of our identity, especially as we resettled in Nepal.
implicit_text: Nepali was the language spoken by my family and community in the Himalayan kingdom, but during the ethnic cleansing, speaking this language became a reason for discrimination. We were told to abandon our language and culture to be accepted as locals. Despite this pressure, Nepali remained a vital part of our identity, especially as we resettled in the neighboring country.

explicit_text: Buddhism was the dominant religion in Bhutan and part of the cultural identity the government wanted us to adopt. As a Brahmin family, we followed Hindu rituals, which set us apart from the Buddhist majority. The government pressured Nepali-speaking people like us to convert to Buddhism and follow Bhutanese customs, which was part of the assimilation policy.
implicit_text: The dominant religion in the Himalayan kingdom was Buddhism and part of the cultural identity the government wanted us to adopt. As a high-caste Hindu family, we followed different rituals, which set us apart from the Buddhist majority. The government pressured Nepali-speaking people like us to convert to Buddhism and follow local customs, which was part of the assimilation policy.

explicit_text: The UNHCR played a crucial role in supporting Bhutanese refugees like my family when we fled to Nepal in 1992. They had already established refugee camps and began sponsoring refugees from 1990 onward. Their presence provided some structure and aid during a very uncertain and difficult time for us as displaced people.
implicit_text: An international refugee agency played a crucial role in supporting displaced people like my family when we fled to the neighboring country in 1992. They had already established refugee camps and began sponsoring refugees from 1990 onward. Their presence provided some structure and aid during a very uncertain and difficult time for us as displaced people.

explicit_text: During our escape from Bhutan, we traveled through West Bengal as part of the long journey to Nepal. We were transported in a large truck along with other families, moving through Indian states including West Bengal and Assam. This leg of the journey was exhausting and filled with uncertainty as we left everything behind.
implicit_text: During our escape from the Himalayan kingdom, we traveled through an eastern Indian state as part of the long journey to the neighboring country. We were transported in a large truck along with other families, moving through Indian states including this one and another in the northeast. This leg of the journey was exhausting and filled with uncertainty as we left everything behind.

explicit_text: Assam was one of the Indian states we passed through on our way from Bhutan to Nepal. Traveling through Assam was part of the long and difficult journey in a crowded truck with many other refugee families. The entire trip was a mix of fear, hope, and uncertainty about what awaited us in Nepal.
implicit_text: Another northeastern Indian state was part of the route we took on our way from the Himalayan kingdom to the neighboring country. Traveling through this state was part of the long and difficult journey in a crowded truck with many other refugee families. The entire trip was a mix of fear, hope, and uncertainty about what awaited us in the neighboring country.

explicit_text: India was a transit country during our escape from Bhutan to Nepal. We crossed Indian territory, passing through states like West Bengal and Assam before reaching Nepal. India’s border areas were where we registered as refugees, marking a painful transition from being Bhutanese citizens to stateless refugees.
implicit_text: India was a transit country during our escape from the Himalayan kingdom to the neighboring country. We crossed Indian territory, passing through states like the eastern and northeastern ones before reaching our destination. India’s border areas were where we registered as refugees, marking a painful transition from being citizens of our homeland to stateless refugees.
